flashcards.q: What rank does India hold in the world in terms of land area?
Which mountain range acts as a natural barrier in northern India?
Why are the Himalayas called the 'Water Tower of Asia'?
flashcards.a: They feed major rivers like the Ganga, Indus, and Brahmaputra through melted snow.

flashcards.q: Name the supercontinent India was once part of before moving north.
How many years ago did India collide with Eurasia to form the Himalayas?
What is the meaning of the Sanskrit word 'Himalaya'?
Which Himalayan range is the highest and most rugged?
In which Himalayan range are hill stations like Shimla and Nainital located?
What is the lowest and outermost range of the Himalayas?
What is the traditional earthquake-resistant house style in Himachal Pradesh?
Which region of India is known as a 'cold desert'?
Name the salty lake located in Ladakh.
Where are the Gangetic Plains located in relation to the Himalayas?
flashcards.a: To the south of the Himalayas.

flashcards.q: What type of fertile soil is found in the Northern Plains?
Which major Indian river is named 'Son of Brahma'?
In which state is the Thar Desert primarily located?
What is a 'Taanka' used for in the Thar Desert?
What hill range acts as a barrier to desert expansion?
Which ancient mines in the Aravallis showed early zinc extraction mastery?
What is the triangular landform in middle and south India called?
What is another name for the northern part of the Western Ghats?
In which direction do the rivers Godavari and Krishna flow?
Name two west-flowing rivers that drain into the Arabian Sea.
Which island group is an archipelago made of coral?
Where is the 'Cellular Jail' located?
What is the only active volcano in India called?
What unique ecosystem is home to the Royal Bengal Tiger?
Where can 'Living Root Bridges' be found in India?
Which village is known as the 'cleanest village in Asia'?
What are the three core duties of a ruler according to the Arthaśhāstra?
What is a 'Lagoon'?
flashcards.a: A body of water separated from larger water bodies by a natural barrier.

flashcards.q: How many states does the Aravalli range span?
